original: The film professor promulgated that both in terms of sex appeal and political intrigue, Sean Connery’s James Bond was superior to Roger Moore’s.
revised: The professor promulgated his view that Connery's Bond surpassed Moore's.

original: The dark storm clouds visible on the horizon suggested that the weather would not be propitious for sailing.
revised: Storm clouds on the horizon suggested weather not propitious for sailing.

original: Erma’s old-fashioned parents believed that her mini-skirt lacked the propriety expected of a “nice” girl.
revised: Her parents felt the mini-skirt lacked the propriety expected of a nice girl.

original: Among Nigel’s protean talents was his ability to touch the tip of his nose with his tongue.
revised: Among Nigel's protean talents was touching his nose with his tongue.

original: The musician had never taken a guitar lesson in his life, making his prowess with the instrument even more incredible.
revised: With no lessons, his prowess on guitar seemed all the more incredible.

original: After losing a fortune in a stock market crash, my father vowed to practice greater prudence in future investments.
revised: After the crash, my father vowed to show greater prudence in investing.

original: David’s mother was shocked by the discovery of prurient reading material hidden beneath her son’s mattress.
revised: She was shocked to find prurient magazines hidden under her son's mattress.

original: The judge demanded order after the lawyer’s puerile attempt to object by stomping his feet on the courtroom floor.
revised: The judge demanded order after the lawyer's puerile foot-stomping objection.

original: Even when scolded, the recalcitrant young girl simply stomped her foot and refused to finish her lima beans.
revised: The recalcitrant child stomped her foot and refused to eat her beans.

original: Reclusive authors such as J.D. Salinger do not relish media attention and sometimes even enjoy holing up in remote cabins in the woods.
revised: Reclusive authors like Salinger shun publicity and often retreat to remote cabins.

original: The burglar gained entrance because the security guard, remiss in his duties, forgot to lock the door.
revised: The burglar entered because the guard, remiss in duty, forgot the lock.

original: Fiona’s renunciation of red meat resulted in weight loss, but confused those people who thought she’d been a vegetarian for years.
revised: Fiona's renunciation of red meat surprised friends who thought she ate fish.

original: The cat, after eating an entire can of tuna fish, reposed in the sun and took a long nap.
revised: After a can of tuna, the cat reposed in the sun and napped.

original: Jean’s cruel and reprehensible attempt to dump her boyfriend on his birthday led to tears and recriminations.
revised: Jean's reprehensible plan to dump her boyfriend on his birthday caused tears.

original: Because the governor woke up in a particularly good mood, he granted hundreds of reprieves to prisoners.
revised: In a good mood, the governor granted hundreds of reprieves to prisoners.

original: Lara reproved her son for sticking each and every one of his fingers into the strawberry pie.
revised: Lara reproved her son for sticking every finger into the strawberry pie.

original: Kwame made a strong case for an extension of his curfew, but his mother repudiated it with a few biting words.
revised: Though Kwame argued eloquently, his mother repudiated his plea for a later curfew.

original: After the most reputable critic in the industry gave the novel a glowing review, sales took off.
revised: After a reputable critic praised the novel, sales rose sharply.

original: The company rescinded its offer of employment after discovering that Jane’s resume was full of lies.
revised: The company rescinded the job offer after discovering lies on Jane's resume.

original: Igor the Indomitable had quite a reservoir of strengh and could lift ten tons, even after running 700 miles, jumping over three mountains, and swimming across an ocean.
revised: Even after a marathon, Igor drew on a vast reservoir of strength.

original: With a resolute glint in her eye, Catherine announced that she was set on going to college in New York City even though she was a little frightened of tall buildings.
revised: With a resolute gleam in her eye, Catherine vowed to attend college in New York.

original: As the media worked itself into a frenzy, the publicist hurriedly retracted his client’s sexist statement.
revised: As the press frenzy grew, the publicist retracted his client's sexist remark.

original: After missing the curfew set by the court for eight nights in a row, Marcel’s freedom of movement was revoked.
revised: After Marcel missed curfew eight nights, the court revoked his freedom of movement.

original: While some giggled at the ribald joke involving a parson’s daughter, most sighed and rolled their eyes.
revised: Some giggled at the ribald joke about the parson's daughter; most rolled their eyes.

original: Terry liked to ruminate while sitting on the banks of the river, staring pensively into the water.
revised: Terry liked to ruminate on the riverbank, staring pensively into the water.

original: Oliver concocted an elaborate ruse for sneaking out of the house to meet his girlfriend while simultaneously giving his mother the impression that he was asleep in bed.
revised: Oliver devised a ruse to sneak out while convincing his mother he was asleep.

original: With remarkable sagacity, the wise old man predicted and thwarted his children’s plan to ship him off to a nursing home.
revised: With remarkable sagacity, the old man foresaw his children's plan to send him away.

original: The sanctimonious Bertrand delivered stern lectures on the Ten Commandments to anyone who would listen, but thought nothing of stealing cars to make some cash on the side.
revised: The sanctimonious Bertrand lectured on commandments yet stole cars for extra cash.

original: Polly reacted to any bad news with a sanguine smile and the chirpy cry, “When life hands you lemons, make lemonade!”
revised: Polly met bad news with a sanguine smile and her usual lemonade motto.

original: Satiated after eating far too much turkey and stuffing, Liza lay on the couch watching football and suffering from stomach pains.
revised: Satiated after too much turkey, Liza lay on the couch watching football.

original: Two hours after breaking up with Russell, Suzanne thought of the perfect scathing retort to his accusations.
revised: Hours later, Suzanne thought of a scathing retort to Russell's accusations.

original: When Bruno heard the scurrilous accusation being made about him, he could not believe it because he always tried to be nice to everyone.
revised: Bruno could not believe the scurrilous rumor, since he tried to be kind to everyone.

original: Stephen Greenblatt’s essays on Shakespeare proved to be seminal, because they initiated the critical school of New Historicism.
revised: Greenblatt's essays on Shakespeare were seminal, launching the school of New Historicism.

original: With a coy smile, the guest on the blind-date show announced that he considered himself a very sensual person.
revised: With a coy smile, the blind-date guest said he was a sensual person.

original: Paul found drinking Coke, with all the little bubbles bursting on his tongue, a very sensuous experience.
revised: Paul found the bubbles of Coke on his tongue a sensuous experience.

original: Jason believed that maintaining his sobriety in times of crisis was the key to success in life.
revised: Jason believed maintaining sobriety during crises was key to success in life.

original: Jim, laid up in bed with a nasty virus, enjoyed the solicitous attentions of his mother, who brought him soup and extra blankets.
revised: Sick in bed, Jim enjoyed his mother's solicitous attentions: soup and extra blankets.

original: Using a spurious argument, John convinced the others that he had won the board game on a technicality.
revised: John used a spurious argument to convince others he had won on a technicality.

original: Charles’s stolid reaction to his wife’s funeral differed from the passion he showed at the time of her death.
revised: Charles's stolid manner at his wife's funeral contrasted with his grief when she died.

original: After partaking of the surfeit of tacos and tamales at the All-You-Can-Eat Taco Tamale Lunch Special, Beth felt rather sick.
revised: After a surfeit of tacos at the lunch special, Beth felt quite sick.

original: After speaking to only one of the students, the teacher was able to surmise what had caused the fight.
revised: After one interview, the teacher could surmise what had caused the fight.

original: The surreptitious CIA agents were able to get in and out of the house without anyone noticing.
revised: The surreptitious agents entered and left the house without anyone noticing.

original: Some see the people in the cabinet as the president’s closest advisors, but others see them as sycophants.
revised: Some call cabinet aides advisors; others dismiss them as mere sycophants.

original: As time passed and the history professor continued to drone on and on, the lecture became increasingly tedious.
revised: As the professor droned on, the lecture grew increasingly tedious.

original: The department heads tore down the arguments in other people’s theses, but Johari’s work proved to be quite tenable.
revised: While others' theses fell apart, Johari's argument proved surprisingly tenable.

original: In college, I used to carry around an anatomy book that was the heaviest tome in my bag.
revised: In college I carried an anatomy book, the heaviest tome in my bag.

original: Because virtually everyone in Palm Beach is a tourist, the population of the town is quite transient.
revised: Because most residents of Palm Beach are tourists, the population is quite transient.

original: According to the school newspaper’s merciless theater critic, Pacific Coast High’s rendition of the musical Oklahoma was a travesty of the original.
revised: The school critic called the production of Oklahoma a travesty of the original.

original: Feeling great trepidation, Anya refused to jump into the pool because she thought she saw a shark in it.
revised: With great trepidation, Anya refused to jump into the pool with the shark.

original: Keith thought of himself as being very learned, but everyone else thought he was trite because his observations about the world were always the same as David Letterman’s.
revised: Keith fancied himself learned, but others found his observations trite and repetitive.

original: This club doesn’t really attract the dangerous types, so why was that bouncer being so truculent?
revised: Why was the bouncer so truculent when this club rarely attracts dangerous types?

original: After winning the derby, the jockey truncated the long speech he had planned and thanked only his mom and his horse.
revised: After winning the derby, the jockey truncated his speech, thanking only his mother.

original: Sir Marcus’s chivalry often contrasted with the turpitude he exhibited with the ladies at the tavern.
revised: Sir Marcus's chivalry contrasted with the turpitude he showed toward women at the tavern.

original: Luka had an uncanny ability to know exactly what other people were thinking. She also had an uncanny ability to shoot fireballs from her hands.
revised: Luka had an uncanny knack for knowing exactly what other people were thinking.

original: The unctuous receptionist seemed untrustworthy, as if she was only being helpful because she thought we might give her a big tip.
revised: The unctuous receptionist seemed helpful only because she expected a large tip.

original: The last thing Lindsay wanted was for Lisa to upbraid her again about missing the rent payment.
revised: Lindsay dreaded Lisa upbraiding her again about missing the rent payment.

original: The rogue army general tried to usurp control of the government, but he failed because most of the army backed the legally elected president.
revised: The general tried to usurp the government but failed when the army backed the president.

original: Everyone in the world wants to live in a utopia, but no one can agree how to go about building one.
revised: Everyone wants to live in a utopia, yet no one agrees how to build one.

original: I prefer a definite answer, but my boss kept vacillating between the distinct options available to us.
revised: I prefer firm decisions, but my boss kept vacillating among the options before us.

original: Beyonce realized that the lyrics she had just penned were completely vacuous and tried to add more substance.
revised: Beyonce saw the lyrics she had penned were vacuous and tried to add substance.

original: Yoko’s chemistry lab partner was asleep during the experiment and could not validate the accuracy of her methods.
revised: Yoko's lab partner slept through the experiment and could not validate her methods.

original: Each wire in the engineering exam was variegated by color so that the students could figure out which one was which.
revised: Each wire on the exam was variegated by color so students could tell them apart.

original: Thanks to her Chanel makeup, Shannen was able to maintain a veneer of perfection that hid the flaws underneath.
revised: With Chanel makeup, Shannen kept a veneer of perfection that hid her flaws.

original: It took the verbose teacher two hours to explain the topic, while it should have taken only fifteen minutes.
revised: The verbose teacher spent two hours on a topic that needed fifteen minutes.

original: Do you know if the Mexican tortilla is a vestige of some form of Aztec corn-based flat bread?
revised: Is the Mexican tortilla a vestige of some Aztec corn-based flatbread?

original: The vicissitudes of daily life prevent me from predicting what might happen from one day to the next.
revised: The vicissitudes of daily life keep me from predicting what tomorrow may bring.

original: After the Watergate scandal, almost any story written about President Nixon sought to vilify him and criticize his behavior.
revised: After Watergate, stories about Nixon often sought to vilify him and his conduct.

original: The attorney had no chance of vindicating the defendant with all of the strong evidence presented by the state.
revised: With the state's strong evidence, the attorney could not vindicate the defendant.

original: The vindictive madman seeks to exact vengeance for any insult that he perceives is directed at him, no matter how small.
revised: The vindictive madman seeks revenge for every slight, no matter how small.

original: Even though Lydia has studied piano for many years, she’s only average at it. She’s no virtuoso, that’s for sure.
revised: Though Lydia studied piano for years, she remains average, no virtuoso at all.

original: Jack ran away as soon as his father found out, knowing he would be vituperated for his unseemly behavior.
revised: Jack fled, knowing his father would vituperate him for his unseemly behavior.

original: After growing tired of the superficial world of high-fashion, Edwina decided to devote herself to a new vocation: social work.
revised: Tired of fashion, Edwina chose a new vocation: dedicated social work.

original: My roommate can’t get over her breakup with her boyfriend and now just wallows in self-pity.
revised: My roommate still wallows in self-pity after her boyfriend broke up with her.

original: Don’t be so afraid of his wrath because his influence with the president is already beginning to wane.
revised: Do not fear his wrath; his influence with the president has begun to wane.

original: Though they were not the strongest of the Thundercats, wily Kit and Kat were definitely the most clever and full of tricks.
revised: Though not the strongest Thundercats, wily Kit and Kat were the most clever tricksters.

original: After such a long, frustrating day, I was grateful for Chris’s winsome attitude and childish naivete.
revised: After a frustrating day, I welcomed Chris's winsome attitude and childish naivete.

original: Did you really want to incur her wrath when she is known for inflicting the worst punishments legally possible?
revised: Did you want to incur her wrath when she imposes the harshest legal punishments?

original: If he were any more zealous about getting his promotion, he’d practically live at the office.
revised: If he were more zealous about promotion, he would practically live at the office.

original: I was too nice to tell Nelly that she had reached the absolute zenith of her career with that one hit of hers.
revised: I never told Nelly that her one hit marked the zenith of her career.

original: If not for the zephyrs that were blowing and cooling us, our room would’ve been unbearably hot.
revised: Without the zephyrs cooling us, our room would have been unbearably hot.
